name: DeepSeek-V4-Flash (Think High)
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts (284B total / 13B active), hybrid Compressed Sparse Attention + Heavily Compressed Attention
note: Smaller, cheaper sibling MoE released alongside V4-Pro on the same day; fast default mode. (middle reasoning-effort tier, called 'Think High' on the official HF model-card benchmark table).

results.notes: Think High mode, per HF model card
Terminal-Bench 2.0, Think High mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).
BrowseComp Pass@1, Think High mode. Cross-verified identical value in DeepSeek-V4 tech report Table 7 (arXiv:2606.19348).

verificationNotes:
Confirmed via official DeepSeek api-docs news post and the deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash HF model card (284B total/13B active, MIT, 1M context, same three-tier benchmark table format as V4-Pro).

2026-07-16: Checked all five missing core benchmarks (mmlu, math, aime, humaneval, mmmu) against (1) the official deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-V4-Flash and DeepSeek-V4-Pro HF model cards plus the DeepSeek-V4 tech report (arXiv:2606.19348) — whose instruct/reasoning-tier comparison table (Non-Think/Think High/Think Max) reports MMLU-Pro, SimpleQA-Verified, Chinese-SimpleQA, GPQA Diamond, HLE, LiveCodeBench, Codeforces, HMMT 2026 Feb, IMOAnswerBench, Apex, MRCR, Terminal-Bench, SWE-Verified/Pro/Multilingual, BrowseComp, MCPAtlas, Toolathlon — but never plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or MMMU for any reasoning tier. (Plain MMLU/MATH/HumanEval scores do exist in the card's separate *base-model* table for "DeepSeek-V4-Flash-Base," a distinct pretrained, non-instruct checkpoint that is not this corpus entry and is not otherwise tracked, so those numbers were not borrowed across model identity.) (2) Independent trackers: Artificial Analysis's DeepSeek V4 Flash Intelligence Index breakdown uses a newer benchmark set (GDPval-AA, tau3-Banking, Terminal-Bench, SciCode, HLE, GPQA Diamond, CritPt, AA-Omniscience, AA-LCR) that also excludes MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval/MMMU; Epoch AI's benchmarking hub does not list DeepSeek V4 Flash at all; Vals.ai shows only an aggregate accuracy index with no benchmark-level breakdown found. (3) Sibling-model cross-reference: all five other DeepSeek-V4 family corpus entries (Flash Non-Think, Flash Think Max, Pro Non-Think, Pro Think High, Pro Think Max) likewise carry zero results for these five slugs, consistent with a deliberate family-wide choice to report MMLU-Pro instead of legacy MMLU, and HMMT-2026/IMOAnswerBench/Apex instead of legacy AIME/MATH for the reasoning-tuned variants. MMMU additionally does not apply: this model's capabilities.vision is false (text-only), so a multimodal benchmark requiring image understanding was never evaluated. All five gaps are treated as a genuine, expected absence rather than a data hole.

2026-07-17 (round 2 — deeper pass targeting six new source types not checked in round 1, per assignment): Re-chased mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval. Result: no credible independent number found anywhere for any of the four; all remain a genuine, corroborated absence. Detail per source type:

(1) Stanford HELM — fetched HELM Lite's core-scenarios leaderboard (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/lite, which includes an "MMLU (Massive Multitask Language Understanding)" column and a separate "MATH" column) and HELM Capabilities' leaderboard (crfm.stanford.edu/helm/capabilities, MMLU-Pro/GPQA/Omni-MATH columns) directly in-browser, full tables read. Neither leaderboard contains any DeepSeek-V4-family row at all — HELM Lite's most recent DeepSeek entries are "DeepSeek v3" (MMLU-EM 0.803) and "DeepSeek LLM Chat (67B)"; HELM Capabilities' most recent are "DeepSeek-R1-0528" and "DeepSeek v3". A further search for HELM Instruct turned up only v3/v3.1 DeepSeek mentions. Conclusion: HELM has not evaluated DeepSeek-V4-Flash (or V4-Pro) in any of its Lite/Capabilities/Instruct suites, so this channel cannot supply MMLU or MATH for this model.

(2) arXiv / Semantic Scholar — searched arxiv.org and the web repeatedly for a baseline-comparison table citing "DeepSeek-V4-Flash" alongside MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. Found one tangential hit, EpiQAL (arXiv:2601.03471, "Benchmarking Large Language Models in Epidemiological Question Answering and Reasoning"), which benchmarks a "V4-Flash-Thinking" variant — but only on its own custom epidemiology QA benchmark (0.926/0.703/0.666 EM on its own subsets), not on MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. No paper was found reporting the four target scores for this model. Semantic Scholar's API (api.semanticscholar.org) returned HTTP 429 (rate-limited) on two direct-fetch attempts, so that specific programmatic channel couldn't be queried, but broad web search covering Semantic-Scholar-indexed content surfaced nothing additional.

(3) GitHub eval-harness run logs — checked the ktransformers serving doc (doc/en/DeepSeek-V4-Flash.md: inference throughput only, zero eval scores), the HenryZ838978/deepseek-harness protocol-compatibility repo (behavioral/API-quirk probes only, no results/ directory or results.json), and a third-party compilation (croppedhead/research/RESEARCH-deepseek-v4-flash-benchmarks.md) — which itself only re-aggregates the same vendor LiveCodeBench/Terminal-Bench/SQL numbers already sourced from BenchLM/HF/DeepSeek docs (some marked "estimated") and contains zero MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval entries. No committed lm-evaluation-harness (or equivalent) run against this model's API was located.

(4) Other leaderboards/aggregators — OpenRouter's DeepSeek V4 Flash model page benchmark panel sources exclusively from Artificial Analysis (GPQA Diamond, HLE, IFBench, tau2-Bench Telecom, AA-LCR, GDPval-AA, CritPt, SciCode, AA-Omniscience) — the same substituted benchmark set round 1 already found, still no MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval. Scale AI's SEAL leaderboard carries no DeepSeek-V4 entry of any variant (confirmed via web search: "Scale's SEAL leaderboard has no DeepSeek V4 result"). LiveBench.ai's 2026-06-25 release does list an unqualified "DeepSeek V4 Flash" row (open, overall 65.5, Mathematics 79.6, cost $0.016/successful task) — but LiveBench's Mathematics/Reasoning/Coding columns are its own contamination-resistant composite tasks, not the legacy Hendrycks MATH or AIME datasets, and the row doesn't disambiguate Non-Think/Think-High/Think-Max, so it is not usable as a citation for this corpus's "math"/"aime" slugs. livecodebench.github.io's current time-windowed leaderboard (8/1/2024-5/1/2025) has no DeepSeek-V4 row at all (window predates the model's April 2026 release; only R1-0528 and V3 appear). Chatbot Arena/LMArena category pages surface only Elo rankings ("DeepSeek V4 Flash (thinking) ranked #10 open model / #47 overall in Text Arena"), not raw percentage benchmark scores. PapersWithCode and vellum.ai's Open Source LLM Leaderboard were also checked; vellum.ai explicitly states it excludes MMLU as saturated/outdated and lists only HLE/GPQA/SWE-bench/BrowseComp for this model. Neither had MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval.

(5) Non-English-language sources — ran a Chinese-language query combining the model name with MMLU/跑分/测试 terms; results surfaced only the same English aggregator pages (BenchLM, Lambda, InsiderLLM) re-reporting the identical MMLU-Pro/HF-card figures. No independent Chinese/Japanese/Korean blog, forum, or community leaderboard post with a plain MMLU, MATH, AIME, or HumanEval score for this model was found.

(6) Sibling models — re-confirmed via OpenRouter's DeepSeek model-family listing and general web search that DeepSeek V4 Pro's marketing copy claims "strong performance across knowledge, math, and software engineering benchmarks" but supplies no comparison table with actual MMLU/MATH/AIME/HumanEval numbers for either Pro or Flash — consistent with round 1's finding that all DeepSeek-V4 family corpus entries carry zero results for these four slugs.

Cross-cutting corroboration: the redreamality.com "Field Guide to 2026 LLM Benchmarks" piece independently confirms the industry-wide mechanism behind this absence — "Artificial Analysis removed MMLU-Pro, AIME 2025, and LiveCodeBench from Intelligence Index v4.0 in January precisely because frontier models had pushed them past the point where scores could discriminate" — and BenchLM.ai itself excludes DeepSeek V4 Flash from its public ranking "because it still lacks enough non-generated benchmark coverage to rank safely." All six new source types were checked (exceeding the required minimum of three); none yielded a citable number. mmlu, math, aime, and humaneval remain omitted_no_evidence — no scores added this round.
